title: Melissa Berdjani
content:
People.ContactEmail mberdjani@mccarthy.ca
Melissa is an articling student at McCarthy Tétrault. She obtained a law degree from the Université de Sherbrooke in 2024. She holds a Diploma of College Studies (D.E.C.) in Law, Society and Justice from Collège de Maisonneuve, where she received a scholarship for achieving the highest overall grade point average in her program. She previously completed three cooperative internships at the firm as part of her university program.
While completing her law degree, Melissa founded and led the University of Sherbrooke’s Labour Law Committee. She also worked as a research assistant and grader in constitutional law. Throughout her academic journey, she was named to the Dean’s Honour Roll for all three years of her law degree. Melissa also completed an internship at Legal Aid in the health law department and contributed to the community through legal clinics.
In her free time, Melissa enjoys practicing yoga, playing board games, and painting.
- Blog Post Cannabis and Safety-Sensitive Positions: Air Transat's Zero-Tolerance Policy Meets the Reasonableness Test June 4, 2026
- Blog Post Will the Canada Labour Code prohibit non-competition clauses? June 4, 2026
